constraints.analysis_summary: After Round 1 with an empty guess and zero feedback, we have no information about the secret code. All colors remain possible, and no positions or constraints have been established.

After Round 2, we know the secret contains exactly 3 of the 5 guessed colors, none in the guessed positions, and a fourth color from outside the set. The two unselected colors from the guess are impossible, but we don't know which. The secret remains largely unknown.

After Round 3, combined with Round 2, we have narrowed the secret to exactly 4 colors: one from {red, blue}, two from {green, yellow, white}, and one from {black, purple}. Round 2 tells us that none of the three colors from its guess are at their guessed positions (green not at pos2, yellow not at pos3, red not at pos0, blue not at pos1; white has no position constraint). Round 3 tells us that exactly two of the four guessed positions (0:green,1:yellow,2:white,3:black) are correct, and the third present color (either the remaining one from those four or purple) is misplaced. No specific colors or positions are locked yet.

After Round 4, we have narrowed the secret to a set of possibilities. White is locked at position 2. Purple is confirmed present. The other two colors are one from {red, blue} and one from {green, yellow} (the other B color is white). The arrangement of these three colors in positions 0, 1, 3 has five viable candidates: (1) [green, red, white, purple], (2) [green, purple, white, red], (3) [purple, yellow, white, red], (4) [green, purple, white, blue], (5) [blue, yellow, white, purple], (6) [purple, yellow, white, blue] — but note (6) is symmetric to (5) in structure. All these satisfy the 0 correct positions for Round 4 and previous constraints. Black is impossible. Further rounds are needed to pinpoint the exact secret.

After round 5, we have uniquely determined three of the four secret colors and their positions: green at pos0, yellow at pos1, white at pos3. The fourth color at pos2 is unknown and must be a color not yet guessed in any round (i.e., not red, blue, green, yellow, white, black, purple, or orange). All previous rounds' constraints are satisfied by this arrangement. The remaining task is to identify the missing color.

After round 6, the secret code is exactly solved. The guess at positions 0-3 matched perfectly: green at pos0, yellow at pos1, yellow at pos2, white at pos3. The fifth guess element (white at pos4) is extra and does not affect positions. The secret contains two yellows, one green, one white. All previous rounds' clues are consistent with this solution.

After round 7, the secret code is confirmed once again as [green, yellow, yellow, white]. This round provides no new information beyond a consistency check. The solution is fully known with high confidence.

Step 2: IDENTIFY LOCKED POSITIONS - No positions are locked because there are no guessed colors to match.

Step 4: IDENTIFY IMPOSSIBLE COLORS - No colors can be ruled out because none were tested.
Step 5: IDENTIFY UNKNOWNS - The entire secret of 4 positions and all possible colors are unknown. No information has been gained.

constraints.analysis_summary: After Round 1 with an empty guess and zero feedback, we have no information about the secret code. All colors remain possible, and no positions or constraints have been established.

After Round 2, we know the secret contains exactly 3 of the 5 guessed colors, none in the guessed positions, and a fourth color from outside the set. The two unselected colors from the guess are impossible, but we don't know which. The secret remains largely unknown.

After Round 3, combined with Round 2, we have narrowed the secret to exactly 4 colors: one from {red, blue}, two from {green, yellow, white}, and one from {black, purple}. Round 2 tells us that none of the three colors from its guess are at their guessed positions (green not at pos2, yellow not at pos3, red not at pos0, blue not at pos1; white has no position constraint). Round 3 tells us that exactly two of the four guessed positions (0:green,1:yellow,2:white,3:black) are correct, and the third present color (either the remaining one from those four or purple) is misplaced. No specific colors or positions are locked yet.

After Round 4, we have narrowed the secret to a set of possibilities. White is locked at position 2. Purple is confirmed present. The other two colors are one from {red, blue} and one from {green, yellow} (the other B color is white). The arrangement of these three colors in positions 0, 1, 3 has five viable candidates: (1) [green, red, white, purple], (2) [green, purple, white, red], (3) [purple, yellow, white, red], (4) [green, purple, white, blue], (5) [blue, yellow, white, purple], (6) [purple, yellow, white, blue] — but note (6) is symmetric to (5) in structure. All these satisfy the 0 correct positions for Round 4 and previous constraints. Black is impossible. Further rounds are needed to pinpoint the exact secret.

After round 5, we have uniquely determined three of the four secret colors and their positions: green at pos0, yellow at pos1, white at pos3. The fourth color at pos2 is unknown and must be a color not yet guessed in any round (i.e., not red, blue, green, yellow, white, black, purple, or orange). All previous rounds' constraints are satisfied by this arrangement. The remaining task is to identify the missing color.

After round 6, the secret code is exactly solved. The guess at positions 0-3 matched perfectly: green at pos0, yellow at pos1, yellow at pos2, white at pos3. The fifth guess element (white at pos4) is extra and does not affect positions. The secret contains two yellows, one green, one white. All previous rounds' clues are consistent with this solution.

After round 7, the secret code is confirmed once again as [green, yellow, yellow, white]. This round provides no new information beyond a consistency check. The solution is fully known with high confidence.
